system.description:
Discipline: Waijia
Skill: Grapple against Parry
Type: Normal
Qi: 3

You unfurl a portion of your sleeve, robe or fabric at your opponents, entangling them in elaborate wrappings. Make a Grapple roll against Parry. On a success you can launch strips of fabric at your foes, by up to 10 feet per Rank of Waijia (provided you have enough cloth to do so), and can use this to Restrain them (see Restrain rules in CHAPTER ONE under GRAPPLE). This allows you to target one foe per Rank of Waijia (or one person per 10 feet). Characters restrained by arms of Silk can attempt to escape as a Skill Action by making a Grapple roll as usual (the entangling silk has an effective Parry of 5 + your Ranks in Waijia). Escaping from Arms of Silk can be attempted as a free action for characters with higher Qi Ranks than the user of the Technique. Cathartic: When used Cathartically you can attempt to choke your opponent (using the Suffocation rules), or squeeze them for 1d10 Damage per level of Qi each round you maintain your hold. The entangling silk has an effective Parry of 7+ your Ranks in Waijia on a Cathartic use.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
system.catharticEffect: When used Cathartically you can attempt to choke your opponent (using the Suffocation rules), or squeeze them for 1d10 Damage per level of Qi each round you maintain your hold. The entangling silk has an effective Parry of 7+ your Ranks in Waijia on a Cathartic use.
